publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday August 19, 2020 at 03:48

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The William Smith Mason Papers contain correspondence, invoices, bookseller descriptions and scrapbooks documenting Mason's acquisition of Benjamin Franklin-related books and manuscripts. The collection spans the dates 1901 to 1935, but the bulk of the material dates from between 1922 and 1931. Major correspondents include the bookseller and collector William J. Campbell; George S. Eddy; Luther Livingston; the Arthur H. Clark Company; and the Rosenbach Company.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence, 1901-1935; II. Scrapbooks, 1909-1935.

bioghist.head: WILLIAM SMITH MASON (1866-1961)
bioghist.p: William Smith Mason (1866-1961) was an American real estate and railroad executive and collector of historical books and manuscripts, particularly those related to Benjamin Franklin. Born in Millburn, Illinois in 1866, he was graduated from Yale's Sheffield Scientific School with a B.Phil in Engineering in 1889. An active member of many bibliographical and historical societies, including the Grolier Club and the American Philosophical Society, Mason donated his entire collection of books, pamphlets, ephemera and manuscripts by and related to Benjamin Franklin to Yale University Library in 1936. He died in Rancho Santa Fe, California, in January 1961 at the age of 94.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William Smith Mason Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The William Smith Mason Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I, Correspondence, is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

did.unittitle: Dobell, P. J. and A. E.
Van de Graaf Dopere, H. F.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II, Scrapbooks,is arranged largely chronologically bh the earliest entry in each scrapbook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The scrapbooks in series II contain invoices, receipts and business correspondence documenting Mason's purchases of Franklin-related materials.
